name: Fuhong Beef Noodle
why: MERGE_EXISTING

name: Liao Beef Noodle
why: Bib Gourmand 2018-19; 40-year clear-broth master; lunch-only queue institution (DanielFoodDiary, Michelin).

name: Niu Dian Beef Noodles
why: Bib Gourmand 3 straight years; hotel-chef 24-hour broth; Ximending must-eat (DanielFoodDiary, Taiwan Obsessed).

name: Halal Chinese Beef Noodles
why: Bib Gourmand 7 straight years; 1957 halal institution, clear broth + beef pancake (Michelin).

name: Mai Mien Yen Tsai
why: 80-year, 3rd-generation shop; NT$20 noodles and legendary crispy hongshao pork; multi-year Bib Gourmand.

name: Chen's Cold Noodles
why: 50-year liang mian icon; miso-egg-fishball soup combo inventor; celebrity-endorsed late-night staple.

name: Yi Mian Wang
why: ~90-year Dadaocheng yi-mian shop; sweet-chili dry noodles, wontons, old-school shaved ice.

name: Dragon Inn Dumplings
why: 1969 evening-only dumpling and luwei queue legend; Taipei Quarterly dumpling guide top-3.

name: Dongmen Dumpling House
why: ~70-year northern-Chinese dumpling hall on Yongkang St; the local's pick (Taipei Times, Taiwan Obsessed).

name: Zhou Ji Pork Congee
why: 70-year Wanhua landmark; NT$15 rou zhou plus one of Taipei's three great crispy hongshao pork (Taiwan Scene, Johor Kaki).

name: Xiao Li Zi Congee Buffet
why: 35-year king of congee street: unlimited sweet-potato porridge, ~100 sides, open 5pm-6am.

name: World Soy Milk King
why: The 1955 originator of Yonghe soy-milk culture, 24h; the original Taiwanese breakfast shop (Taiwan Obsessed).

name: San Yuan Hao
why: Century-old (1920) institution; lean lu rou fan + shark-fin geng combo; 500 Bowls list.

name: Wan Lin Lu Rou Fan
why: Guidebook-famous 50-year lu rou fan hall beloved by tour groups (Taipei Navi).

name: Yi Jia Zi
why: Bib Gourmand every year since 2020; 60-year braised pork belly rice and gua bao by Qingshui Temple.

name: Jin Yuan Pork Chop
why: Since 1973 in Wannian basement; Taipei's benchmark fried pork chop, free rice/soup refills (DanielFoodDiary).

name: Yuan Huan Pien Oyster Omelette
why: Since 1965, Ningxia's most-queued stall; multi-year Michelin listing, Jensen Huang's famous stop.

name: Fang Chia Shredded Chicken Rice
why: Bib Gourmand 2019-2022; NT$50 Chiayi-style chicken rice, Ningxia's rice king.

name: Liu Yu Zai Fried Taro Balls
why: 5-year Bib Gourmand; 40-year stall, egg-yolk taro balls with Ningxia's longest queue.

name: Chen Dong Herbal Ribs Soup
why: Raohe's first yao dun paigu; Bib Gourmand 5 straight years 2018-2022.

name: Shi Yuan Salt & Pepper Chicken
why: Since 1984, PTT-crowned birthplace of garlic yansuji; Shida night market's defining fry stall.

name: A-Tu Sesame Oil Chicken
why: 40+ year stall widely called Taipei's best sesame oil chicken; winter queues like concerts (Johor Kaki).

name: Liang Ji Chiayi Chicken Rice
why: Since 1975, office-worker legend for turkey rice with half-cooked egg.

name: 50 Lan
why: Taiwan's default homegrown boba since 1994; locals' everyday order (DanielFoodDiary, Klook).

name: Chun Shui Tang
why: The teahouse credited with inventing pearl milk tea in 1987; essential (DanielFoodDiary, Klook).

name: Truedan
why: Shilin-born originator of hand-cooked brown sugar boba (2010), now global.

name: KEBUKE
why: Cult aged-black-tea chain with ~300 stores; top-10 list regular.

name: Smoothie House
why: Yongkang St's iconic mango snowflake ice; CNN-ranked Taiwanese food (DanielFoodDiary).

name: Bingzan
why: SEASONAL (Apr-Oct only). Aiwen mango snow ice; pilgrimage-level for Japanese and Korean visitors.

name: Jingimoo
why: Dongmen's artisan rose and burnt-matcha shaved ice; every Taipei dessert roundup features it.

name: Shuanglian Yuanzai Soup
why: Third-generation 1951 shop; seared mochi repeatedly named Taipei's must-eat tangyuan.

name: Sao Douhua
why: Famous seasonal fruit douhua (mango, strawberry, watermelon); expanded to Japan.

name: Snow King Ice Cream
why: 1947 institution with 73 handmade flavors (basil, pork knuckle, kaoliang).

name: Dong Qu Fen Yuan
why: 40-year tapioca-pearl dessert legend with 70 toppings; on every classic-dessert list.

name: Summer Tree Sweet
why: Dihua Street's almond-tofu snow ice icon; first in local almond-tofu rankings.

name: Ice in Wonderland
why: Since 1995, all-handmade Alishan aiyu and grass jelly, open till 3am; Tonghua must-eat.

name: Shuang Yue Food
why: 7-yr Bib Gourmand herbal chicken soup; even Morris Chang eats here (Michelin).

name: Yuanfang Guabao
why: 1955 Huaxi St gua bao legend, house-made buns, 5+ consecutive Bib Gourmand years.

name: Shi Jia Fresh Meat Tangyuan
why: 1964 Yansan Night Market Hakka savory tangyuan; multi-year Bib Gourmand.

name: Jianhong Beef Noodles
why: Ximending 24hr budget beef-noodle icon; cheap huge portions; 2018 first-edition Bib Gourmand.

name: Atong Abao Four Herbs Soup
why: Taipei's most-reviewed si-shen-tang, est. 1977, open till 5am; cabbies' favorite.

name: Liu Mama Rice Balls
why: One of Taipei's big-three fantuan, ~30 fillings, purple-rice signature; daily queues.

name: Qingdao Soy Milk
why: Rare surviving oven-baked shaobing breakfast shop; a living museum with morning queues.

name: Dingyuan Soy Milk
why: 60-yr breakfast institution; xiaolongbao, crab-shell pastry, salty soy milk.

name: Laopai Beef Ramen King
why: 70+ yr hidden Chengzhong-market shop; rustic hand-pulled mala beef noodles.

name: Lin Jia Dry Noodles
why: 60-yr shop selling only lard-scallion dry noodles (NT$37) + fishball soup; 2025 Bib Gourmand.

name: Ajiao Braised Pork
why: Taiping Market's fabled fried hongshaorou across six cuts; called Taipei's strongest; takeout only.
